texto: That one,” Ferox said, pointing at Johann with a claw. “I can see he’s communicated like that before and I think I can speak to his mind. Let me see.”“After a moment, Johann broke from the line and approached the dragon.“You did ask me to come closer, didn’t you?” he asked.“Yes, I can speak to this one. He can be my rider.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tom Larcombe: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “That one,” Ferox said, pointing at Johann with a claw. “I can see he’s communicated like that bef…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, communication, metaphor pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de metaphor.

Let me see.”“After a moment, Johann broke from the line and approached the dragon.“You did ask me to come closer, didn’t you?” he asked.“Yes, I can speak to this one. He can be my rider.

texto: Is there anyone nearby you can speak to without words?”“I don’t know. I only know that there are those whose minds I can speak with that, when I show them how, speak back to my mind as well.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Tom Larcombe que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Is there anyone nearby you can speak to without words?”“I don’t know. I only know that there are …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, metaphor, speak, com foco em reflexao.
